skill.name: slo-implementation
skill.description: Define Service Level Indicators, Service Level Objectives, error budgets, alerts, dashboards, and review practices for reliability programs.
skill.summary: Implement practical SLOs with measurable SLIs, error budgets, burn-rate alerts, and review routines.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as shell execution and the word description as weak cryptography. Manual review found these are documentation examples and YAML labels, with no executable scripts, network calls, prompt injection, or credential access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks are markdown code fences, inline file references, and examples for PromQL, YAML, dashboards, and related skills. They do not instruct the agent to execute shell commands or run untrusted input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present, but semantic review shows documentation formatting rather than command execution. No shell invocation, script file, or execution directive is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Reported as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits occur on YAML or alert annotation keys named description. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing routine, encryption configuration, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner appears to match the substring in description rather than an algorithm use. Manual review found no DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic implementation.

content.user_title: Implement SLOs and Error Budget Alerts
content.value_statement: Teams struggle to define reliability goals that reflect user experience. This skill guides SLI selection, SLO targets, error budgets, alerts, and reviews.
actual_capabilities: Explains how SLIs, SLOs, SLAs, and error budgets relate to each other.
Provides example availability, latency, and durability SLI formulas.
Shows Prometheus recording rule patterns for SLI and SLO measurements.
Outlines burn-rate alerting rules for fast and slow error budget consumption.
Suggests dashboard sections for SLO compliance, trends, and budget status.
Defines weekly, monthly, and quarterly SLO review activities.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include executable scripts or automation.
Referenced resource files are not present in the packaged skill.
Example queries must be adapted to each service metric schema.
It does not validate Prometheus or Grafana configurations automatically.

use_cases.title: Set reliability targets for a service
use_cases.description: Define user-facing availability and latency objectives with measurable SLIs and practical targets.

use_cases.title: Create error budget policies
use_cases.description: Translate an SLO target into budget thresholds, release guidance, and reliability review actions.

use_cases.title: Design SLO dashboards and alerts
use_cases.description: Plan Prometheus recording rules, burn-rate alerts, and Grafana views for SLO operations.

prompt_templates.title: Choose starter SLIs
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single service
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose availability and latency SLIs for a public API. Ask for missing service details before recommending targets.

prompt_templates.title: Draft an SLO proposal
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an SLO proposal for our checkout service using current performance, user expectations, and business impact.

prompt_templates.title: Plan error budget alerts
prompt_templates.prompt: Design fast and slow burn-rate alerts for a 99.9 percent availability SLO over a 28 day window.

prompt_templates.title: Review an SLO program
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit our current SLO program, identify weak SLIs, review alert noise risks, and recommend a quarterly improvement plan.

output_examples.input: Define SLOs for an API that must feel reliable to paying customers.
output_examples.output: A concise SLO plan with availability and latency SLIs, target percentages, measurement windows, and rationale for each objective.

output_examples.input: We have incidents but no error budget process.
output_examples.output: An error budget policy with remaining-budget thresholds, release guidance, escalation points, and review cadence.

output_examples.input: Our alerts are noisy and do not reflect user impact.
output_examples.output: A burn-rate alert strategy that separates urgent budget burn from slower reliability degradation.

best_practices: Start with user-facing journeys before adding internal technical SLIs.
Use multiple indicators so availability, latency, and durability risks are visible.
Review SLOs regularly and adjust targets when user expectations or systems change.
anti_patterns: Do not set every SLO to 100 percent availability.
Do not alert on raw symptoms without connecting them to user impact.
Do not create SLO targets without an error budget policy.

faq.question: What does this skill help define?
faq.answer: It helps define SLIs, SLOs, error budgets, burn-rate alerts, dashboards, and review practices.

faq.question: Does it run Prometheus or Grafana commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation patterns and examples that users must adapt and apply manually.

faq.question: Can it choose an SLO target for me?
faq.answer: It can recommend target ranges after considering user expectations, current performance, and reliability cost.

faq.question: Is this only for web APIs?
faq.answer: No. The same concepts can apply to storage, batch jobs, data pipelines, and other user-facing services.

faq.question: What monitoring tools does it reference?
faq.answer: It references Prometheus recording rules and Grafana dashboard structure as implementation examples.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare service goals, critical user journeys, current metrics, incident history, and business reliability requirements.
